{{also|Word|worð}} ==English== ===Alternative forms=== * {{alter|en|wurd||eye dialect}} * {{alter|en|worde||obsolete}} ===Pronunciation=== * {{IPA|en|/wɜːd/|a=RP}} * {{IPA|en|/wɜɹd/|a=GA}}, {{enPR|wûrd}} * {{audio|en|En-us-word.ogg|a=GA}} * {{IPA|en|/wəɹd/|[wɚ(ɹ)d]|a=CA}} * {{IPA|en|/wɛːd/|a=Humberside,Teesside,square-nurse}} * {{IPA|en|/wøːd/|a=NZ}} * {{IPA|en|/wʌrd/|a=Scotland}} * {{IPA|en|/weːd/|a=Scouse,square-nurse}} * {{homophones|en|whirred|;|wared}} * {{rhymes|en|ɜː(ɹ)d|s=1}} ===Etymology 1=== [[File:About- General sign.ogv|thumb| The word ''about'' signed in [[American Sign Language]].]] {{root|en|ine-pro|*werh₁-|*dʰeh₁-}} From {{inh|en|enm|word}}, from {{inh|en|ang|word}}, from {{inh|en|gmw-pro|*word}}, from {{inh|en|gem-pro|*wurdą|t=word}}, from {{inh|en|ine-pro|*werdʰh₁om|t=word}}, from {{m|ine-pro|*werh₁-|t=to say, speak}}. {{doublet|en|verb|verve|vort}}; further related to {{m|en|vrata}}. {{col-top|2|cog}} Cognate with {{cog|sco|wird|t=word}}, {{cog|frr|uurd}}, {{m|frr|Uurt}}, {{m|frr|wurd}}, {{m|frr|wårde}}, {{m|frr|wür|t=word}}, {{cog|stq|Woud}}, {{m|stq|Wud|t=word}}, {{cog|fy|wurd|t=word}}, {{cog|gsw|wort}}, {{m|gsw|wourd}}, {{m|gsw|wuart}}, {{m|gsw|wòrt}}, {{m|gsw|wört|t=word}}, {{cog|bar|boart}}, {{m|bar|bort}}, {{m|bar|bört}}, {{m|bar|Wurt|t=word}}, {{cog|cim|bóart}}, {{m|bar|bort|t=word}}, {{cog|nl|woord|t=word}}, {{cog|de|Wort|t=word}}, {{cog|nds-de|Woord}}, {{m|nds-de|Woort|t=word}}, {{cog|li|waord}}, {{m|li|Woërt|t=word}}, {{cog|lb|Wuert|t=word}}, {{cog|mhn|bourt|t=word}}, {{cog|wym|wiüt}}, {{m|wym|wuyt|t=word}}, {{cog|yi|וואָרט|t=word}}, {{cog|da,nb,nn,sv|ord|t=word}}, {{cog|ovd|uord|t=word}}, {{cog|fo,is,gmq-scy|orð|t=word}}, {{cog|gmq-gut|ård|t=word}}, {{cog|got|𐍅𐌰𐌿𐍂𐌳|t=word}}; also {{cog|grc|ῥητός|t=spoken, stipulated}}; also {{cog|la|verbum|t=word}}, {{cog|xum|𐌖𐌄𐌓𐌚𐌀𐌋𐌄|t=temple}}, {{cog|grc|εἴρω|t=to say, speak, tell}}, {{cog|ltg|vuords|t=name; word}}, {{cog|lv|vārds|t=name; word}}, {{cog|lt|var̃das|t=name}}, {{cog|ru|врать|t=to lie}}, {{cog|sh|ва̏рати}}, {{m|sh|vȁrati|t=to cheat, deceive, trick, swindle; to be mistaken}}, {{cog|hy|հորջորջել|t=to call, name}}, {{cog|ask|vīri|t=word}}, {{cog|bsh|vëri}}, {{m|bsh|viri|t=word}}, {{cog|prn|veri}}, {{m|prn|verī}}, {{m|prn|vëre}}, {{m|prn|vëri|t=word}}, {{cog|sa|व्रत|t=command; law; will}}. {{col-bottom}} ====Noun==== {{en-noun|~}} # {{senseid|en|Q8171}}{{lb|en|semantics}} The smallest unit of language that has a particular meaning and can be expressed by itself; the smallest [[discrete]], [[meaningful]] unit of language. {{qualifier|contrast ''[[morpheme]]''.}} #* {{quote-book|en|year=1897|author=Ouida|chapter=The New Woman|title=An Altruist and Four Essays|page=239|passage=But every '''word''', whether written or spoken, which urges the woman to antagonism against the man, every '''word''' which is written or spoken to try and make of her a hybrid, self-contained opponent of men, makes a rift in the lute to which the world looks for its sweetest music.}} #* {{quote-book|en|year=1986|author=David Barrat|title=Media Sociology|isbn=041505110X|page=112|passage=The '''word''', whether written or spoken, does not look like or sound like its meaning — it does not resemble its signified. We only connect the two because we have learnt the code — language. Without such knowledge, 'Maggie' would just be a meaningless pattern of shapes or sounds.}} #* {{quote-book|en|year=2009|author=Jack Fitzgerald|title=Viva La Evolucin|isbn=055719833X|page=233|passage=Brian and Abby signed the '''word''' ''clothing'', in which the thumbs brush down the chest as though something is hanging there. They both spoke the '''word''' ''clothing''. Brian then signed the '''word''' for ''change'', {{...}}}} #* {{quote-journal|en|date=2013-06-14|author=[http://www.guardian.co.uk/profile/sam-leith Sam Leith]|volume=189|issue=1|page=37|magazine=w:The Guardian Weekly|title=[http://www.guardian.co.uk/books/2013/may/23/holy-shit-history-swearing-mohr Where the profound meets the profane]|passage=Swearing doesn't just mean what we now understand by "dirty words". It is entwined, in social and linguistic history, with the other sort of swearing: vows and oaths. Consider for a moment the origins of almost any '''word''' we have for bad language – "profanity", "curses", "oaths" and "swearing" itself.}} ## The smallest discrete unit of spoken language with a particular meaning, composed of one or more [[phoneme]]s and one or more [[morpheme]]s ##* {{quote-journal|en|year=1894|author=Alex. R. Mackwen|title=The Samaritan Passover|magazine=Littell's Living Age|issue=6|volume=1|passage=Then all was silent save the voice of the high priest, whose '''words''' grew louder and louder, {{...}}}} ##* {{RQ:Churchill Celebrity|chapter=4|passage=Mr. Cooke at once began a tirade against the residents of Asquith for permitting a sandy and generally disgraceful condition of the roads. So roundly did he vituperate the inn management in particular, and with such a loud flow of '''words''', that I trembled lest he should be heard on the veranda.}} ##* '''2006 Feb. 17''', Graham Linehan, ''[[w:The IT Crowd|The IT Crowd]]'', Season 1, Episode 4: ##*: I can't believe you want me back.
You've got Jen to thank for that. Her '''words''' the other day moved me deeply. Very deeply indeed.
Really? What did she say.
Like I remember! Point is it's the effect of her '''words''' that's important. ## The smallest discrete unit of written language with a particular meaning, composed of one or more [[letter]]s or [[symbol]]s and one or more [[morpheme]]s ##* {{RQ:Shakespeare Hamlet|2|2|text=Polonius: What do you read, my lord?
Hamlet: '''Words''', '''words''', '''words'''.}} ##* {{quote-book|en|year=2003|author=Jan Furman|title=Toni Morrison's Song of Solomon: A Casebook|isbn=0195146352|page=194|passage=The name was a confused gift of love from her father, who could not read the '''word''' but picked it out of the Bible for its visual shape, {{...}}}} ##* {{quote-book|en|year=2009|author=Stanislas Dehaene|title=Reading in the Brain: The New Science of How We Read|isbn=1101152400|passage=Well-meaning academics even introduced spelling absurdities such as the “s” in the '''word''' “island,” a misguided Renaissance attempt to restore the etymology of the [unrelated] Latin word ''insula''.}} ## A discrete, meaningful unit of language approved by an [[authority]] or [[native speaker]] (''compare [[non-word]]''). ##* {{quote-book|en|year=1896|author=w:Israel Zangwill|title=Without Prejudice|page=21|passage=“Ain’t! How often am I to tell you ain’t ain’t a '''word'''?”}} ##* {{quote-book|en|year=1999|author=Linda Greenlaw|title=The Hungry Ocean|publisher=Hyperion|page=11|passage=''Fisherwoman'' isn’t even a '''word'''. It’s not in the dictionary.}} # Something like such a unit of language: #: {{hypernyms|en|syntagma}} ## A [[sequence]] of [[letters]], characters, or [[sound]]s, considered as a [[discrete]] [[entity]], though it does not necessarily belong to a language or have a meaning. ##* {{quote-book|en|year=1974|title=Thinking Goes to School: Piaget's Theory in Practice|isbn=0199839077|page=183|passage=In still another variation, the nonsense '''word''' is presented and the teacher asks, "What sound was in the beginning of the word?" "In the middle?" and so on. The child should always respond with the phoneme; he should not use letter labels.}} ##* {{quote-book|en|author=w:Matt Ridley|title=Genome|year=1999|publisher=Harper Collins|page=301|pageurl=https://archive.org/details/isbn_9780965213677_f7s0/page/301/mode/1up|isbn=978-0-9652136-7-7|passage=All 15.5 million ‘'''words'''’ (or so–the exact length depends on the repeat sequences, which vary greatly) in the twenty-second chapter of the human autobiography have been read and written down in English letters: 47 million As, Cs, Gs and Ts.}} ##* {{quote-book|en|year=2003|title=How To Do Everything with Your Tablet PC|isbn=0072227710|page=278|passage=I wrote a nonsense '''word''', "umbalooie," in the Input Panel's Writing Pad. Input Panel converted it to "cembalos" and displayed it in the Text Preview pane.}} ##* {{quote-book|en|year=2006|title=Scribal Habits and Theological Influences in the Apocalypse|isbn=3161491122|page=141|passage=Here the scribe has dropped the με from καθημενος, thereby creating the nonsense '''word''' καθηνος.}} ##* {{quote-book|en|year=2013|title=The Cognitive Neuropsychology of Language|isbn=1317859979|page=91|passage=If M. V. has sustained impairment to a phonological output process common to reading and repetition, we might anticipate that her mispronunciations will partially reflect the underlying phonemic form of the nonsense '''word'''.}} ## {{lb|en|telegraphy}} A unit of [[text]] equivalent to five [[character]]s and one [[space]]. {{defdate|from 19th c.}} ## {{senseid|en|Q625642}} {{lb|en|computing}} A fixed-size group of [[bit]]s handled as a unit by a machine and which can be stored in or retrieved from a typical [[register]] (so that it has the same size as such a register). {{defdate|from 20th c.}} ##* {{quote-book|en|year=1997|author=John L. Hennessy|coauthors=David A. Patterson|title=Computer Organization and Design|section=§3.3|url=|page=109|text=The size of a register in the MIPS architecture is 32 bits; groups of 32 bits occur so frequently that they are given the name ''word'' in the MIPS architecture.|publisher=Morgan Kaufmann Publishers, Inc.|location=San Francisco, California|edition=2nd}} ## {{senseid|en|16bits}} {{lb|en|computing}} With regards to Intel or Intel-compatible hardware and/or in the context of Windows programming, a group of exactly 16 [[bit]]s regardless of the actual processor capabilities; a fossilized unit referring to the small word size of historical CPUs. {{defdate|from 20th c.}} ## {{lb|en|computer science}} A [[finite]] [[string]] that is [[not]] a [[command]] or [[operator]]. {{defdate|from 20th c.}} ## {{lb|en|group theory}} A [[group]] [[element]], expressed as a [[product]] of group elements. # The fact or act of [[speaking]], as opposed to taking [[action]]. {{defdate|from 9th c}}. #* {{quote-book|en|year=1811|author=w:Jane Austen|title=w:Sense and Sensibility|passage={{...}} she believed them still so very much attached to each other, that they could not be too sedulously divided in '''word''' and deed on every occasion.}} #* {{quote-journal|en|date=8 September 2004|author=Richard Williams|work=The Guardian|passage=As they fell apart against Austria, England badly needed someone capable of leading by '''word''' and example.}} # {{lb|en|now|rare|_|outside certain phrases}} Something that someone said; a [[comment]], [[utterance]]; [[speech]]. {{defdate|from 10th c.}} #* {{RQ:KJV|Matthew|26|75|text=And Peter remembered the '''word''' of Jesus, which said unto him, Before the cock crow, thou shalt deny me thrice.}} #* {{quote-book|en|year=1847|author={{w|Alfred, Lord Tennyson|Alfred Tennyson}}|title={{w|The Princess (Tennyson poem)|The Princess}}|passage=She said; but at the happy '''word''' "he lives", / My father stooped, re-fathered, o'er my wound.}} #* {{quote-book|en|year=1853|author=w:Charles Dickens|title=w:Bleak House|passage=There is only one other point on which I offer a '''word''' of remark.}} #* {{quote-journal|en|date=April 1 1945|author=Sebastian Haffner|work=The Observer|passage="The Kaiser laid down his arms at a quarter to twelve. In me, however, they have an opponent who ceases fighting only at five minutes past twelve," said Hitler some time ago. He has never spoken a truer '''word'''.}} #* {{quote-book|en|year=2011|author=w:David Bellos|title=Is That a Fish in Your Ear?|publisher=Penguin|year_published=2012|page=126|passage=Despite appearances to the contrary [...] dragomans stuck rigidly to their brief, which was not to translate the Sultan's words, but his '''word'''.}} #* {{quote-book|en|year=2011|author=John Lehew (senior)|title=The Encouragement of Peter|isbn=1615074708|page=108|passage=In what sense is God's Word living? No other '''word''', whether written or spoken, has the power that the Bible has to change lives.}} # {{lb|en|obsolete|_|outside certain phrases}} A [[watchword]] or rallying cry, a verbal signal (even when consisting of multiple words). #: {{ux|en|[[mum's the word|mum's the '''word''']]}} #* {{quote-book|en|year=1592|author=William Shakespeare|title={{w|Richard III (play)|Richard III}}|passage=Our ancient '''word''' of courage, fair Saint George, inspire us with the spleen of fiery dragons!}} #* {{quote-book|en|year=c. 1623|year_published=1647|author=John Fletcher; William Rowley|title=w:The Maid in the Mill|edition_plain=published in the first Beaumont and Fletcher folio)|section=scene 3|passage=I have the '''word''' : sentinel, do thou stand; {{...}}}} # {{lb|en|obsolete}} A [[proverb]] or [[motto]]. #* {{quote-book|en|year=1499|author=w:John Skelton|title=The Bowge of Court|passage=Among all other was wrytten in her trone / In golde letters, this '''worde''', whiche I dyde rede: / ''Garder le fortune que est {{l|fr|mal|mauelz}} et {{l|fr|bon|bone}}''.}} #* {{quote-book|en|year=1599|author=Ben Jonson|title=w:Every Man out of His Humour|passage=Let the '''word''' be 'Not without mustard'. Your crest is very rare, sir.}} #* {{quote-book|en|year=1646|author={{w|Joseph Hall (bishop)|Joseph Hall}}|title=The Balm of Gilead|passage=The old '''word''' is, 'What the eye views not, the heart rues not.'}} # {{senseid|en|news, tidings}} {{lb|en|uncountable}} [[news|News]]; [[tidings]]. {{defdate|from 10th c.}} #: {{ux|en|Have you had any '''word''' from John yet?}} #: {{ux|en|I've tried for weeks to get '''word''', but I still don't know where she is or if she's all right.}} #* {{RQ:King James Version|book=Matthew|chapter=2|verse=13|passage={{...}} Arise and take the young childe, and his mother, and flee into Egypt, and bee thou there vntill I bring thee '''word''': for Herode will seeke the young childe, to destroy him.}} #* {{RQ:Orwell Animal Farm|1|passage='''Word''' had gone round during the day that old Major, the prize Middle White boar, had had a strange dream on the previous night and wished to communicate it to the other animals.}} # An [[order]]; a [[request]] or [[instruction]]; an expression of will. {{defdate|from 10th c.}} #: {{ux|en|He sent '''word''' that we should strike camp before winter.}} #: {{ux|en|Don't fire till I give the '''word'''}} #: {{ux|en|Their mother's '''word''' was law.}} # {{senseid|en|oath promise}}A [[promise]]; an [[oath]] or [[guarantee]]. {{defdate|from 10th c.}} #: {{syn|en|promise}} #: {{ux|en|I give you my '''word''' that I will be there on time.}} # A brief [[discussion]] or [[conversation]]. {{defdate|from 15th c.}} #: {{ux|en|I managed to murmur some '''word'''s of consolation as I left the funeral.}} #: {{ux|en|Can I have a '''word''' with you?}} # {{lb|en|meiosis}} A minor [[reprimand]]. #: {{ux|en|I had a '''word''' with him about it.}} # {{lb|en|in the plural}} ''See'' '''[[words]]'''. #: {{ux|en|There had been '''words''' between him and the secretary about the outcome of the meeting.}} # {{lb|en|theology|sometimes '''[[Word]]'''}} Communication from [[God]]; the [[message]] of the Christian [[gospel]]; the [[Bible]], [[Scripture]]. {{defdate|from 10th c.}} #: {{syn|en|word of God|Bible}} #: {{ux|en|Her parents had lived in Botswana, spreading the '''word''' among the tribespeople.}} # {{lb|en|theology|sometimes '''[[Word]]'''}} [[Logos]], [[Christ]]. {{defdate|from 8th c.}} #: {{syn|en|God|Logos}} #* {{RQ:Tyndale NT|John|I|text=And that '''worde''' was made flesshe, and dwelt amonge vs, and we sawe the glory off yt, as the glory off the only begotten sonne off the father, which '''worde''' was full of grace, and verite.}} #* {{RQ:Tennyson In Memoriam|XXXVI|56|And so the '''Word''' had breath, and wrought
⁠With human hands the creed of creeds
⁠In loveliness of perfect deeds,
More strong than all poetic thought; {{...}}}} =====Usage notes===== * In English and other languages with a tradition of space-delimited writing, it is customary to treat "word" as referring to any sequence of characters delimited by spaces. However, this is not applicable to languages such as [[Chinese]] and [[Japanese]], which are normally written without spaces, or to languages such as [[Vietnamese]], which are written with spaces delimiting [[syllable]]s. In computing, the size (length) of a word, while being fixed in a particular machine or processor family design, can be different in different designs, for many reasons. See {{w|Word (computer architecture)}} for a full explanation. =====Synonyms===== * [[vocable]]; ''see also'' '''[[Thesaurus:word]]''' =====Derived terms===== {{col|en|title=words, types of word{{,}} de Deus|f}} * Romanian: {{tt+|ro|cuvânt|n}} * Russian: {{t|ru|сло́во бо́жье|n}} * Slovak: {{tt|sk|slovo božie|n}}, {{tt|sk|božie slovo|n}} * Telugu: {{tt+|te|వాణి}} }} {{trans-bottom}} ====Verb==== {{en-verb}} # {{lb|en|transitive}} To [[say]] or [[write]] (something) using particular words; to [[phrase]] (something). #: {{syn|en|express|phrase|put into words|state}} #: {{ux|en|I’m not sure how to '''word''' this letter to the council.}} # {{lb|en|transitive|obsolete}} To [[flatter]] with words, to [[cajole]]. #: {{syn|en|blandish|butter up|inveigle|Thesaurus:coax}} #* {{quote-book|en|year=1607|author=William Shakespeare|title=Anthony and Cleopatra|section=act 5, scene 2|passage=He '''words''' me, girls, he '''words''' me, that I should not / be noble to myself.}} # {{lb|en|transitive}} To [[ply]] or [[overpower]] with words. #* {{quote-book|en|date=November 30 1621|author=James Howell|title=letter to Francis Bacon, from Turin|passage={{...}} if one were to be '''worded''' to death, Italian is the fittest Language [for that task]}} #* {{quote-journal|en|date=April 1829|title=Webster's Dictionary|magazine=The North American Review|volume=28|page=438|passage={{...}} if a man were to be worded to death, or stoned to death by words, the High-Dutch were the fittest [language for that task].}} # {{lb|en|transitive|rare}} To [[conjure]] with a word. #* {{quote-text|en|year=c. 1645-1715|author=w:Robert South|title=Sermon on Psalm XXXIX. 9 |passage=Against him {{...}} who could '''word''' heaven and earth out of nothing, and can when he pleases '''word''' them into nothing again.}} #* {{quote-journal|en|year=1994|title=Liminal Postmodernisms|journal=Postmodern Studies|volume=8|page=162|passage="Postcolonialism" might well be another linguistic construct, desperately begging for a referent that will never show up, simply because it never existed on its own and was literally '''worded''' into existence by the very term that pretends to be born from it.}} #* {{quote-book|en|year=2013|author=Carla Mae Streeter|title=Foundations of Spirituality: The Human and the Holy|isbn=0814680712|page=92|passage=The being of each person is '''worded''' into existence in the Word, {{...}}}} # {{lb|en|intransitive|archaic}} To [[speak]], to use words; to [[converse]], to [[discourse]]. #: {{syn|en|chat|jaw|moot|Thesaurus:converse}} #* {{RQ:Keats Lamia|poem=Hyperion|page=181|passage=Thus '''wording''' timidly among the fierce: / "O Father, I am here the simplest voice, {{...}}"}} ("I speak the truth.")}} # {{lb|en|slang|emphatic|AAVE}} {{n-g|An abbreviated form of {{m|en|word up}}; a statement of the acknowledgment of fact with a hint of nonchalant approval.}} #* {{quote-book|en|year=2004|author=Shannon Holmes|title=Never Go Home Again: A Novel|page=218|passage="{{...}} Know what I'm sayin'?" / "'''Word'''!" the other man strongly agreed. "Let's do this — "}} #* {{quote-book|en|year=2007|author=Gabe Rotter|title=Duck Duck Wally: A Novel|page=105|passage="{{...}} Not bad at all, man. Worth da wait, dawg. '''Word'''." / "You liked it?" I asked dumbly, stoned still, and feeling victorious. / "Yeah, man," said Oral B. "'''Word''' up. {{...}}"}} #* {{quote-book|en|year=2007|author=Relentless Aaron|title=The Last Kingpin|page=34|passage="{{...}} I mean, I don't blame you... '''Word'''! {{...}}"}} ====Quotations==== * {{see citations|en}} ====See also==== * [[allomorph]] * [[compound word]] * [[grapheme]] * [[idiomatic]] * [[lexeme]] * [[listeme]] * [[morpheme]] * [[orthographic]] * [[phrase]] * [[set phrase]] * [[syllable]] * [[term]] ===Etymology 2=== Variant of {{m|en|worth||to become, turn into, grow, get}}, from {{inh|en|enm|worthen}}, from {{inh|en|ang|weorþan|t=to turn into, become, grow}}, from {{inh|en|gmw-pro|*werþan}}, from {{inh|en|gem-pro|*werþaną|t=to turn, turn into, become}}. More at {{section link|worth#Verb}}. ====Verb==== {{head|en|verb}} # {{alt form|en|worth|t=to become}}. ====See also==== * [[Appendix:Wordhood]] ===Further reading=== * {{pedia}} ===Anagrams=== * {{anagrams|en|a=dorw|drow}} {{cln|en|autological terms}} {{C|en|Communication}} ==Afrikaans== ===Alternative forms=== * {{alt|af|worde||obsolete|see usage notes below}} ===Etymology=== From {{inh|af|nl|worden}}, from {{inh|af|dum|werden}}, from {{inh|af|odt|werthan}}, from {{inh|af|gem-pro|*werþaną}}. ===Pronunciation=== * {{IPA|af|/vɔrt/}} * {{audio|af|LL-Q14196 (afr)-Oesjaar-word.wav}} ===Verb=== {{af-verb}} # to [[become]]; to [[get]] {{gloss|to change one’s state}} #: {{uxi|af|Ek het ryk '''geword'''.|t=I '''became''' rich.}} #: {{uxi|af|Ek '''word''' ryk.|t=I '''am becoming''' rich.}} #: {{uxi|af|Sy '''word''' beter.|t=She '''is getting''' better.}} # {{non-gloss|Forms the present passive voice when followed by a past participle}} #: {{uxi|af|Die kat '''word''' gevoer.|t=The cat '''is being''' fed.}} ====Usage notes==== * The verb has an archaic preterite {{m|af|werd}}: {{m|af||Die kat werd gevoer.|t=The cat was fed.}} In contemporary Afrikaans the perfect is used instead: {{m|af||Die kat is gevoer.}} * Until the early 20th century this verb still often distinguished the infinitive {{m|af|worde}} from the present tense {{m|af||word}} (as is now still done with {{m|af|[[wees]] ↔ [[is]]}} and {{m|af|[[hê]] ↔ [[het]]}}): {{m|af||Dit moet verbeter worde en dit word ook verbeter.|t=It must be improved and it is being improved.}} {{cln|af|auxiliary verbs|irregular verbs}} ==Chinese Pidgin English== ===Alternative forms=== * {{alter|cpi|𭉉||Chinese spelling}} ===Etymology=== From {{inh|cpi|en|word}}. ===Noun=== {{head|cpi|noun}} # [[#English|word]] #* {{RQ:cpi:Instructor|volume=6|page=78}} #*: {{cpi-q|挨仙{{sic|⿰口仙}}㕭'''𭉉''' |aai1 sin1[sen1] ju1 '''wut3''' |I send you '''word'''. |I will send you '''word'''.}} ==Dutch== ===Pronunciation=== * {{IPA|nl|/ʋɔrt/}} * {{rhymes|nl|ɔrt|s=1}} * {{audio|nl|Nl-word.ogg}} ===Verb=== {{head|nl|verb form}} # {{infl of|nl|worden||bare-verb}} ==Middle English== ===Etymology 1=== From {{inh|enm|ang|word}}, from {{inh|enm|gmw-pro|*word}}, from {{inh|enm|gem-pro|*wurdą}}, from {{inh|enm|ine-pro|*werdʰh₁om}}. {{doublet|enm|verbe}}. ====Alternative forms==== * {{alter|enm|vord|weord|woord|worde|wourd|wurd}} ====Pronunciation==== * {{IPA|enm|/wurd/|/woːrd/}} ====Noun==== {{enm-noun|wordes|pl2=word|pl2_qual=early}} # A {{l|en|word}} {{gloss|separable, discrete linguistic unit}} #* {{quote-book|enm|year=a.1400|author=w:Geoffrey Chaucer|title=w:Troilus and Criseyde|chapter=Book II|line= 22–28|passage= Ȝe knowe ek that in fourme of ſpeche is chaunge / With-inne a thousand ȝeer, and '''wordes''' tho / That hadden pris now wonder nyce and ſtraunge / Us thenketh hem, and ȝet thei ſpake hem so / And ſpedde as wel in loue as men now do|translation=You also know that the form of language is in flux; / within a thousand years, '''words''' / that had currency; really weird and bizarre / they seem to us now, but they still spoke them / and accomplished as much in love as men do now.}} # A [[statement]]; a linguistic unit said or [[written]] by someone: ## A [[speech]]; a formal [[statement]]. ## A [[byword]] or maxim; a short expression of truth. ## A [[promise]]; an [[oath]] or [[guarantee]]. ## A [[motto]]; an [[expression]] associated with a person or people. ## A piece of [[news]] {{gloss|often warning or recommending}} ##* {{RQ:Malory Le Morte Darthur|version=WM|chapter=Morte Arthur|folio=480|verso=yes|folioend=481|passage=My lorde ſeyde ẜ ''Bedwere'' youre cõmaundement ſhaỻ be done, and lyghtly brynge you '''word''' a gayne.|translation="My lord," said Sir Bedivere, "your order will be carried out, and I'll promptly bring you '''news''' [about it] afterwards."}} ## An [[order]] or [[directive]]; something necessary. ## A [[religious]] precept, [[stricture]], or belief. # The act of [[speaking]] {{gloss|especially as opposed to action}}: # [[discourse|Discourse]]; the [[exchange]] of statements. ## The [[basic]], non-figurative reading of something. ## The way one [[speaks]] {{gloss|especially with modifying adjective}} # {{lb|enm|theology}} The [[Logos]] {{gloss|Jesus Christ}} #* {{RQ:Wycliffe NT Lichfield|book=John|chapter=1|verse=1|leaf=44|side=r|column=2|passage=IN þe bigynnyng was þe '''woꝛd''' .· ⁊ þe '''woꝛd''' was at god / ⁊ god was þe '''woꝛd'''|translation=In the beginning was the '''Word''', and the '''Word''' was with God, and God was the '''Word'''.}} # {{lb|enm|rare}} The human faculty of [[language]] as a whole. =====Related terms===== {{col3|enm|bodeword|byword|hereword|mysword|wacche word|worden|wordy|wytword}} =====Descendants===== * {{desc|en|word}} * {{desc|gmw-msc|word|wourd}} ** {{desc|sco|wird|alts=1}} =====References===== * {{R:MED Online|entry= wō̆rd|part of speech=n |id=MED53418 |accessdate=27 February 2020}} ===Etymology 2=== ====Noun==== {{head|enm|noun}} # {{alt form|enm|werde}} {{C|enm|God|Language|Talking}} ==Old English== ===Alternative forms=== * {{alter|ang|ƿord}} * {{alter|ang|uord||Northumbrian}} * {{alter|ang|wyrde}} ===Pronunciation=== * {{ang-IPA|word}} ===Etymology 1=== {{root|ang|ine-pro|*werh₁-|*dʰeh₁-}} From {{inh|ang|gmw-pro|*word}}, from {{inh|ang|gem-pro|*wurdą}}. ====Noun==== {{ang-noun|n|word}} # [[#English|word]] #* {{quote-book|ang|text=''Sē þonne þisne wealsteal · wīse ġeþōhte
ond þis deorce līf · dēope ġeondþenċeð,
frōd in ferðe, · feor oft ġemon
wælsleahta worn, · ond þās '''word''' ācwið:''|t=Then he deeply thinks over this wall-place
and this dark life with wise thought,
shrewd in mind, oft recalls the long bygone
swarm of slaughters, and utters these '''words''':|title=The Wanderer|year=10th century}} # [[speech]], [[utterance]], [[statement]] # {{lb|ang|grammar}} [[verb]] # [[news]], [[information]], [[rumour]] #* '''c. 992''', [[w:Ælfric of Eynsham|Ælfric]], [https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/The_Homilies_of_the_Anglo-Saxon_Church/II "Sermon on the Nativiity of Our Lord"] #*: {{quote|ang|Ðyllīce '''word''' María hēold ārǣfniġende on hire heortan.|Such '''words''' Mary held, pondering them in her heart.}} # [[command]], [[request]] # [[Logos]], the Word of [[God]], the second person of the [[Trinity]], [[Jesus]] =====Declension===== {{ang-decl-noun-a-n|word}} =====Derived terms===== {{col3|ang |bīword |ġylpword |witword |wordfæst |wordiġ |wordriht }} =====Descendants===== * {{desctree|enm|word}} ===Etymology 2=== {{root|ang|ine-pro|*Hwerdʰ-}} {{etymid|ang|thornbush}} {{unk|ang}}. Perhaps ultimately from {{der|ang|ine-pro|*wr̥dʰos|t=sweetbriar}}. {{also|bo꞉ok|Book}} ==English== {{wp}} ===Pronunciation=== * {{enPR|bo͝ok}}, {{IPA|en|/bʊk/}} ** {{audio|en|En-uk-book.ogg|a=UK}} ** {{audio|en|en-us-book.ogg|a=US}} * {{enPR|bo͞ok}} {{IPA|en|/buːk/|aa=some speakers from <> and <>,also,_,NNSE}} * {{IPA|en|/bʉk/|a=Scotland,NI,MLE}} * {{homophones|en|buck|aa=<>, when pronounced /bʊk/}} * {{rhymes|en|ʊk|s=1}} ===Etymology 1=== {{etymon|en|id=Q571|:inh|enm:bok|tree=1}} [[File:Pieni2.jpg|right|thumb|A hard-cover '''book''' ({{senseno|en|Q571}})]] From {{inh|en|enm|bok}}, {{m|enm|book}}, from {{inh|en|ang|bōc}}, from {{inh|en|gmw-pro|*bōk}}, from {{inh|en|gem-pro|*bōks}}. ''Bookmaker'' sense by {{clipping|en|nocap=1}}. {{col-top|2|cog}} Cognate with {{cog|sco|beuk}}, {{m|sco|buik}}, {{m|sco|buke|t=book}}, {{cog|yol|buke|t=book}}, {{cog|frr|Bok}}, {{m|frr|buk}}, {{m|frr|bök|t=book}}, {{cog|stq|Bouk|t=book}}, {{cog|fy|-}}, {{cog|nl|boek|t=book}}, {{cog|gsw|Buech|t=book}}, {{cog|bar|Buach|t=book}}, {{cog|gmw-cfr|Booch}}, {{m|gmw-cfr|Buch|t=book}}, {{cog|de|-}}, {{cog|lb|Buch|t=book}}, {{cog|nds-de|Book|t=book}}, {{cog|li|book}}, {{m|li|Bouk|t=book}}, {{cog|wym|büch|t=book}}, {{cog|yi|בוך|t=book}}, {{cog|da|bog|t=book}}, {{cog|ovd|buok|t=book}}, {{cog|fo|-}}, {{cog|is|bók|t=book}}, {{cog|nb|-}}, {{cog|nn|-}} and {{cog|sv|bok|t=book}}. {{col-bottom}} ====Alternative forms==== * {{alt|en|boke||obsolete}} * {{alt|en|booke||archaic}} ====Noun==== {{en-noun}} # {{sid|en|Q571}} A collection of sheets of paper bound together to hinge at one edge, containing printed or written material, pictures, etc. #* {{RQ:Shakespeare Tempest|act=I|scene=ii|page=3|column=1|passage=Knowing I lou'd my '''bookes''', he furniſhd me / From mine owne Library, with volumes, that / I prize aboue my Dukedome.}} #* {{quote-text|en|year=1962|translator=James East Irby|author=w:Luis Borges|title=w:The Library of Babel |passage=I repeat: it suffices that a '''book''' be possible for it to exist. Only the impossible is excluded. For example: no '''book''' can be a ladder, although no doubt there are '''books''' which discuss and negate and demonstrate this possibility and others whose structure corresponds to that of a ladder.}} #* {{quote-text|en|year=1983|author=Steve Horelick; et al|title=w:Reading Rainbow |passage=I can be anything.
Take a look!
It's in a '''book''':
A reading rainbow.}} #* {{quote-text|en|year=1991|author=w:Stephen Fry|title=[[:w:The Liar (novel)|The Liar]]|page=51 |passage=Trefusis's quarters could be described in one word. '''Books'''. '''Books''' and '''books''' and '''books'''. And then, just when an observer might be lured into thinking that that must be it, more '''books'''... Trefusis himself was highly dismissive of them. ‘Waste of trees,’ he had once said. ‘Stupid, ugly, clumsy, heavy things. The sooner technology comes up with a reliable alternative the better... The world is so fond of saying that '''books''' should be “treated with respect”. But when are we told that ''words'' should be treated with respect?’}} #: {{ux|en|She opened the '''book''' to page 37 and began to read aloud.}} #: {{ux|en|He was frustrated because he couldn't find anything about dinosaurs in the '''book'''.}} # A long work fit for [[publication]], typically [[prose]], such as a [[novel]] or [[textbook]], and typically published as such a bound collection of sheets, but now sometimes electronically as an [[e-book]]. #: {{ux|en|I have three copies of his first '''book'''.}} #* {{quote-web|en|url=https://www.theatlantic.com/technology/archive/2022/12/chatgpt-ai-writing-college-student-essays/672371/|title=The College Essay Is Dead|date=2022-12-06|author=w:Stephen Marche|quotee=w:Sam Bankman-Fried|work=The Atlantic|passage= “I would never read a '''book''',” he once told an interviewer. “I don’t want to say no '''book''' is ever worth reading, but I actually do believe something pretty close to that.”}} #* {{RQ:Pacifik Wonderfool|Freethinker|passage=They read some '''books'''
Sharing their moods
They know everything
Whatever it can mean}} # A major division of a long work. #: {{syn|en|tome|volume}} #: {{ux|en|Genesis is the first '''book''' of the Bible.}} #: {{ux|en|Many readers find the first '''book''' of ''A Tale of Two Cities'' to be confusing.}} # {{senseid|en|gambling}} {{lb|en|gambling}} A record of betting (from the use of a notebook to record what each person has bet). #: {{ux|en|I'm running a '''book''' on who is going to win the race.}} # {{lb|en|informal}} A [[bookmaker]] (a person who takes bets on sporting events and similar); [[bookie]]; [[turf accountant]]. # A convenient collection, in a form resembling a book, of small paper items for individual use. #: {{syn|en|booklet}} #: {{co|en|a '''book''' of stamps}} #: {{co|en|a '''book''' of raffle tickets}} #: {{co|en|a '''book''' of matches ([[matchbook]])}} # {{lb|en|theatre}} The script of a [[musical]] or [[opera]]. #: {{syn|en|libretto}} #* {{quote-text|en|year=2010|author=David Baskerville; Tim Baskerville|title=Music Business Handbook and Career Guide|page=172 |passage=The guild helps ensure that the ownership and control of the music, lyrics, and '''book''' of a show remain in the hands of its authors and composers—not the producers.}} # {{lb|en|usually|in the plural}} Records of the accounts of a business. #: {{syn|en|account|record}} # {{lb|en|legal|colloquial}} A [[book award]], a recognition for receiving the highest grade in a class (traditionally an actual book, but recently more likely a letter or certificate acknowledging the achievement). # {{lb|en|whist}} Six [[trick]]s taken by one side. # {{lb|en|poker slang}} [[four of a kind|Four of a kind]].Weisenberg, Michael (2000) ''[http://www.poker1.com/mcu/pokerdictionary/mculib_dictionary_info.asp The Official Dictionary of Poker].'' MGI/Mike Caro University. {{ISBN|978-1880069523}} # {{lb|en|sports}} A document, held by the referee, of the incidents that happened in a game. # {{lb|en|sports|by extension}} A list of all players who have been booked (received a warning) in a game. #* {{quote-web|en|date=March 2, 2011 |author=Andy Campbell |title=Celtic 1 - 0 Rangers |work=BBC |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/football/9409758.stm |page= |passage=Celtic captain Scott Brown joined team-mate Majstorovic in the '''book''' and Rangers' John Fleck was also shown a yellow card as an ill-tempered half drew to a close.}} # {{lb|en|horse racing}} The list of [[mare]]s that a [[stallion]] will [[breed]] in a given season. # {{lb|en|horse racing}} A list of the [[race]]s that a [[jockey]] is scheduled to ride in. # {{lb|en|cartomancy}} The twenty-sixth [[Lenormand]] card. # {{lb|en|figurative}} Any source of instruction. # {{lb|en|with "the"}} The accumulated body of knowledge passed down among [[black]] [[pimp]]s. #* {{quote-text|en|year=1974|author=Adrienne Lanier Seward|title=The Black Pimp as a Folk Hero|page=11 |passage='''The Book''' is an oral tradition of belief in The Life that has been passed down from player to player from generation to generation.}} #* {{quote-text|en|year=1994|title=Antiquarian Book Monthly|volume=21|page=36 |passage=On the other hand '''The Book''' is an oral tradition containing the rules and principles to be adopted by a pimp who wishes to be a player.}} # {{lb|en|advertising|informal}} A [[portfolio]] of one's previous work in the industry. #* {{quote-text|en|year=2017|author=Nik Mahon|title=Basics Advertising 02: Art Direction|page=8 |passage=Getting your '''book''' (portfolio) organised is the first step, and knowing both what to include, and what to leave out, is an essential step towards achieving that important agency placement.}} #* ''Idea Industry'' (page 27) #*: Your portfolio — your '''book''' — has to be killer. # {{lb|en|chess|uncountable}} The [[sum]] of [[chess]] [[knowledge]] in the [[opening]] or [[endgame]]. #: {{ux|en|The {{w|Opposite-colored bishops endgame|opposite-colored bishops endgame}} is usually a '''book''' draw.}} #: {{co|en|A [[book move|'''book''' move]]}} #: {{co|en|[[out of book|out of '''book''']]}} #* {{quote-web|en|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20230112080030/https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/apr/06/schoolboy-vincent-keymer-wins-grenke-open|work=w:The Guardian|passage=White to move and win. How can he do it? The BK plans a march to h8, eating the f4 pawn en route, for a '''book''' draw.|title=Chess: Schoolboy Vincent Keymer secures shock triumph at Grenke Open|date=2018-04-06|author=w:Leonard Barden}} #* {{quote-book|en|title=How to Swindle in Chess|passage=This seems certain to simplify into a battle between White's king, rook and two pawns against Black's king and rook. In some cases a '''book''' draw is possible. But a '''book''' win is more likely.|year=2020|author=w:Andrew Soltis|isbn=9781849945639|publisher=w:Batsford Books}} # {{lb|en|historical}} A [[package]] of [[silk]]. #* {{quote-journal|en|year=1867|author=John I. Knight|journal=Mechanics Magazine|page=52 |passage=This machinery was provided with a rotary drum with received upon its periphery the "'''books'''" of silk to be operated upon and presented them to travelling, combing, or dressing surfaces. * {{desc|so|buugga|buug||bor=1}} * {{desc|st|buka|bor=1}} {{i|possibly also from Afrikaans {{m|af|boek}}}} * {{desc|zu|ibhuku|bor=1}} {{i|possibly also from Afrikaans {{m|af|boek}}}} {{bottom}} =====Translations===== {{see translation subpage|Noun}} =====See also===== {{col|en |mul:📚 |incunable |scroll |tome |volume }} ===Etymology 2=== From {{inh|en|enm|booken}}, {{m|enm|boken}}, from {{inh|en|ang|bōcian}}, {{m|ang|ġebōcian}}, from the noun (see above). ====Verb==== {{en-verb}} # {{senseid|en|reserve}} {{lb|en|transitive}} To [[reserve]] (something) for future use. #: {{syn|en|reserve}} #: {{ux|en|I want to '''book''' a hotel room for tomorrow night.}} #: {{ux|en|I can '''book''' tickets for the concert next week.}} #* {{quote-journal|en|date=2020 December 2|author=Paul Bigland|title=My weirdest and wackiest Rover yet|journal=Rail|page=68|text=I haven't '''booked''', so I don't have a clue as to whether the service will be busy or not. Supposedly, reservations are compulsory, but I want to find out what would happen if you just turn up.}} # {{lb|en|transitive}} To [[write down]], to [[register]] or [[record]] in a book or as in a book. #: {{syn|en|[[make]] a [[note]] of|[[note]] [[down]]|record|write down}} #: {{ux|en|They '''booked''' that message from the hill.}} # {{lb|en|transitive}} To add a [[name]] to the [[list]] of people who are [[participating]] in something. #: {{syn|en|sign up|register|reserve|schedule|enroll}} #: {{ux|en|I '''booked''' a flight to New York.}} # {{lb|en|law enforcement|transitive}} To [[record]] the name and other details of a suspected offender and the offence for later judicial action. #: {{ux|en|The police '''booked''' him for driving too fast.}} # {{lb|en|sports}} To issue a [[caution]] to, usually a [[yellow card]], or a [[red card]] if a yellow card has already been issued. # {{lb|en|intransitive|slang}} To travel very fast. #: {{syn|en|bomb|hurtle|rocket|speed|shoot|whiz}} #: {{ux|en|He was really '''booking''' until he passed the speed trap.}} # To record [[bet|bets]] as [[bookmaker]]. # {{lb|en|transitive|law student slang}} To receive the highest [[grade]] in a class. #: {{ux|en|The top three students had a bet on which one was going to '''book''' their intellectual property class.}} # {{lb|en|intransitive|slang}} To [[move]] or [[leave]], often hurriedly and abruptly. #: {{ux|en|He was here earlier, but he '''booked'''.}} =====Derived terms===== {{col3|en|title=Terms derived from the verb “book” |block-book|book away |bookable |book in |book into |booking |book off |book on |book up |double-book |overbook |rebook |unbook |underbook }} =====Translations===== {{see translation subpage|Verb}} ===Etymology 3=== From {{inh|en|enm|book}}, {{m|enm|bok}}, from {{inh|en|ang|bōc}}, from {{inh|en|gem-pro|*bōk}}, first and third person singular indicative past tense of {{der|en|gem-pro|*bakaną|t=to bake}}. ====Verb==== {{head|en|verb form}} # {{senseid|en|baked}} {{lb|en|UK|_|dialectal|Northern England}} {{infl of|en|bake||spast}} ===References=== ===Anagrams=== * {{anagrams|en|a=bkoo|Boko|Koob|boko|bòkò|kobo}} {{cln|en|student slang}} {{topics|en|Textual division|Heraldic charges|Printing}} ==Beja== ===Pronunciation=== * {{bej-IPA|bóók}} ===Noun=== {{bej-noun|bóók|g=m|pl=bák}} # {{lb|bej|zoology}} [[buck]] ===References=== * {{R:bej:Reinisch|46}} ==Chinese== ===Alternative forms=== * {{alt|zh|卜|tr=-}} ===Etymology=== From {{bor|yue|en|book}}. ===Pronunciation=== {{zh-pron |c=buk1 |cat=v }} ===Verb=== {{head|zh|verb}} # {{lb|zh|Hong Kong Cantonese|colloquial}} to {{l|en|book}}; to [[reserve]] ====Related terms==== * {{l|zh|booking}} ==French== ===Pronunciation=== * {{audio|fr|LL-Q150 (fra)-Lepticed7-book.wav|a=<> (<>)}} * {{audio|fr|LL-Q150 (fra)-Poslovitch-book.wav|a=<> (<>)}} ===Noun=== {{fr-noun|m}} # [[portfolio]] # [[bookmaker]] ===References=== * {{R:fr:TLFi}} ==Kankanaey== ===Etymology=== Compare {{m+|tl|buhok}}. ===Pronunciation=== {{kne-pr|bo7ók}} ===Noun=== {{kne-noun|boók}} # {{lbl|kne|anatomy}} [[hair]] ===References=== * {{R:kne:Vanoverbergh 1982 anatomy}} ==Limburgish== ===Alternative forms=== * {{alter|li|bouk||Sittard|amongst other dialects}} ** {{alter|li|Bouk||Eupen}} * {{alter|li|Bock||Krefeld}} ===Etymology=== From {{der|li|gml|bôk}}, from {{der|li|osx|bōk}}, from {{inh|li|gmw-pro|*bōk}}, from {{inh|li|gem-pro|*bōks}}. ===Pronunciation=== * {{IPA|li|/boːk/}} * {{hyphenation|li|book}} * {{rhymes|li|oːk|s=1}} ===Noun=== {{head|li|noun|g=n}} # {{also|Appendix:Variations of "fa"}} {{minitoc}} ==Translingual== ===Etymology=== {{clip|mul|en:'''Fa'''rsi}}, from {{der|mul|fa|فارسی|tr='''fâ'''rsi}}, from {{der|mul|ar|فارسي|tr='''fā'''risiyy}}. ===Symbol=== {{mul-symbol}} # {{ISO 639|1}} ===See also=== * {{langcat}} ==English== ===Alternative forms=== * {{alt|en|fah}} ===Pronunciation=== * {{IPA|en|/fɑ/}} * {{audio|en|LL-Q1860 (eng)-Vealhurl-fa.wav|a=Southern England}} * {{rhymes|en|ɑː|s=1}} ===Etymology 1=== From Glover's [[solmization]], from {{inh|en|enm|fa||fourth degree or note of Guido of Arezzo's hexachordal scales}}, {{der|en|it|fa}} in the solmization of Guido of Arezzo, from the first syllable of {{der|en|la|famulī|t=servants}} in the lyrics of the scale-ascending hymn ''{{w|Ut queant laxis}}'' by Paulus Deacon. ====Noun==== {{en-noun}} # {{lb|en|music}} A syllable used in [[solfège]] to represent the fourth diatonic (or sixth chromatic) note of a [[major scale]]. #* {{RQ:Fielding Tom Jones|IV|X|Containing the Arrival of an ''Irish'' Gentleman, with very extraordinary Adventures which ensued at the Inn|pages=11–12|pageref=11|passage=And now Mrs. ''Waters'' (for we muſt confeſs ſhe was in the ſame Bed) being, I ſuppoſe, awakened from her Sleep, and ſeeing two Men fighting in her Bed-chamber, began to ſcream in the moſt violent Manner, crying out Murder! Robbery! and more frequently Rape! which laſt, ſome, perhaps, may wonder ſhe ſhould mention, who do not conſider that theſe Words of Exclamation are uſed by Ladies in a Fright, as '''Fa''', la, la, ra, da, ''&c.'' are in Muſic, only as the Vehicles of Sound, and without any fixed Ideas.}} =====Derived terms===== {{l|en|sol-fa}} =====Translations===== {{trans-top|fourth note of a major scale}} * Arabic: {{t|ar|فَا|m}} * Basque: {{t+|eu|fa}} * Czech: {{t+|cs|fa}} * Dutch: {{t+|nl|fa|m}} * Finnish: {{t+|fi|fa}} * French: {{t+|fr|fa|m}} * Galician: {{t+|gl|fa|m}} * Georgian: {{t+|ka|ფა}} * Hindi: {{t+|hi|म}} * Hungarian: {{t+|hu|fá}} * Italian: {{t+|it|fa|m}} * Japanese: {{t+|ja|ファ|tr=fa}} * Korean: {{t+|ko|파}} * Latvian: {{t+|lv|fa|m}} * Macedonian: {{t|mk|фа}} * Russian: {{t+|ru|фа|n}} * Spanish: {{t+|es|fa}} * Thai: {{t+|th|ฟา}} * Vietnamese: {{t+|vi|pha}} {{trans-bottom}} ====See also==== {{col2|en|title=musical notes|do|la|mi|re|so|ti}} ===Etymology 2=== ====Preposition==== {{en-head|prep}} # {{lb|en|informal}} {{alternative spelling of|en|for}}. #* {{quote-av|en|year=2000|actor={{w|Jared Leto|Jared Leto}}|role=Harry Goldfarb |title=Requiem for a Dream|text=Do ya wanna be a dope fiend '''fa''' krist’s sake?}} =====Derived terms===== {{col|en|fa shizzle}} ====See also==== {{col2|en|title=other terms with "fa", etymologically unrelated|fa cai|fa jin|fa-la|fi fa|Pha̍k-fa-sṳ|}} ===Anagrams=== * {{anagrams|en|a=af|AF|Af|af}} {{cln|en|2-letter words}} ==Asturian== ===Pronunciation=== {{ast-pr}} ===Verb=== {{head|ast|verb form}} # {{lb|ast|obsolete|or|archaic}} {{alt form|ast|fai}} ==Catalan== ===Pronunciation=== * {{ca-IPA}} * {{rhymes|ca|a}} ===Etymology 1=== ====Noun==== {{ca-noun|m|#s}} # {{lb|ca|music}} {{l|en|fa}} {{gloss|fourth note of diatonic scale}} ===Etymology 2=== {{nonlemma}} ====Verb==== {{head|ca|verb form}} # {{ca-verb form of|fer}} ====Preposition==== {{head|ca|preposition}} # [[ago]] #: {{ux|ca|'''fa''' molts anys...|many years '''ago'''...}} ====Interjection==== {{head|ca|interjection}} # {{lb|ca|dialectal}} a particle used in some dialects to emphasize a negative sentence #: {{ux|ca|'''Fa''' que no es veu!|lit=it does that it's not seen!|it’s obvious!}} ==Chichewa== ===Etymology=== {{inh+|ny|bnt-pro|*-kúa}}, potentially from {{inh|ny|alv-pro|*kwwú-||to die}}. Cognate with {{cog|tum|fwa|-fwa|to die}}, {{cog|yao|wa|-wa|to die}}, {{cog|nse|fwa|-fwa|to die}}, {{cog|nyu|fa|-fa|to die}}, {{cog|sw|fa|-fa|to die}}, {{cog|ziw|fa|-fa|to die}}, {{cog|lon|khwa|-khwa|to die}}, {{cog|ngl|khwa|-khwa|to die}}. ===Verb=== {{ny-verb|fa}} # to [[die]] # to have a [[stroke]] # to be [[infertile]] # to [[spend]] or [[give out]] money ====Derived terms==== {{ny-der|appl|caus|pass|neg|vrb=fa mutu|vrb-g=to be amazed, to be stupefied|nom=imfa|nom-g=death|nom2=kuloŵa kufa|nom-g2=the practice of making a widow sleep with a man to put her former husband's soul at rest}} ===References=== * {{R:ny:Paas:2016|page=138}} ==Czech== ===Noun=== {{head|cs|noun}} # {{abbreviation of|cs|firma}} ====Declension==== {{rfinfl|cs|noun}} ==Dutch== ===Etymology=== Borrowed from {{bor|nl|la|fa}}. ===Pronunciation=== * {{IPA|nl|/faː/}} * {{audio|nl|Nl-fa.ogg}} * {{hyphenation|nl|fa}} * {{rhymes|nl|aː}} ===Noun=== {{nl-noun|m|-'s|+}} # {{lb|nl|music}} {{l|en|fa}} {{gloss|fourth diatonic (or sixth chromatic) musical note}} # {{lb|nl|music}} [[F]] (the musical clef or key) ====Derived terms==== * {{l|nl|fasleutel}} ==French== ===Pronunciation=== * {{fr-IPA}} * {{audio|fr|Fr-Paris--fa.ogg|a=Paris}} * {{audio|fr|LL-Q150 (fra)-LoquaxFR-fa.wav|a=<> (<>)}} * {{audio|fr|LL-Q150 (fra)-WikiLucas00-fa.wav|a=<> (<>)}} ===Noun=== {{fr-noun|m|#}} # {{lb|fr|music}} [[fa#English|fa]], the fourth diatonic (or sixth chromatic) note 'F' ====Derived terms==== * {{l|fr|clef de fa}} ===Further reading=== * {{R:fr:TLFi}} ==Galician== ===Noun=== {{gl-noun|m}} #
